fault_hypothesis: The expiry check on line 65 uses strict greater-than (`now > expires_at`) instead of greater-than-or-equal (`now >= expires_at`), so an entry accessed at exactly its expiry timestamp is incorrectly returned as valid instead of expired.

spec.expr: not (now == expires_at__1 and __cbfl_result is not None)

constraints.intent: When now equals expires_at the function must not return a value; the strict > comparison fails this boundary case.
Capture the value of expires_at__1 before the comparison to verify the boundary condition is handled correctly.
After the else branch of the expiry check, now must be strictly less than expires_at; equality should have triggered the then-branch (return None).

spec.expr: not (key in entries and now >= entries[key][1] and __cbfl_result is not None)

constraints.intent: The function must never return a non-None value when now is at or past the expiry timestamp.
